content.text:
[cron:ea289f91-c82f-452c-94b4-6abd6ce87f64 retry-jetton-monitor-build] 检查 Jetton Monitor GitHub Actions 构建状态。如果最新构建失败或未开始，重新触发 Windows 构建工作流。repo: hiyaScott/jetton-monitor
Current time: Monday, March 16th, 2026 — 1:08 PM (Asia/Shanghai)

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.

content.thinking: The user wants me to check the GitHub Actions build status for the jetton-monitor repository and trigger a Windows build if needed. Let me use the GitHub CLI to check the workflow runs.

details.error:
To get started with GitHub CLI, please run:  gh auth login
Alternatively, populate the GH_TOKEN environment variable with a GitHub API authentication token.

Command exited with code 4

content.thinking: GitHub CLI is not authenticated. Let me check if there's a GH_TOKEN environment variable or use a different approach. I might need to check the repository status via the web or check if there's an existing token.
No GH_TOKEN is set. Let me check if there's any GitHub token in the environment or try another approach.
No GitHub token found in environment. Let me check the skill file for GitHub automation to see if there's guidance on authentication.

details.error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, access '/root/.openclaw/workspace/skills/github-automation/SKILL.md'

content.thinking: The skill file doesn't exist. Let me try using the web API directly with curl to check the GitHub Actions status, or check if there's an auth token stored somewhere.
No GitHub CLI configuration. Let me try using curl to access the GitHub API directly. The repository is public so I should be able to read workflow runs without authentication.
